15	import torch
16	from .utils import (
17	    _maybe_fake_quantize_activations,
18	    fast_dequantize,
19	    QUANT_STATE,
20	    get_lora_parameters,
21	    get_lora_parameters_bias,
22	    matmul_lora,
23	    torch_amp_custom_fwd,
24	    torch_amp_custom_bwd,
25	)
26	
27	
28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass

94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass

1769	    self,
1770	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],

1783	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

1785	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1786	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1787	):
1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self
1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
1830	
1831	    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
1832	    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
1833	
1834	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
1835	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
1836

1838	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
1839	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1840	            python_install.wait()

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me create a summary:

## Unsloth LoRA Adapter Weights Handling - Complete Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how LoRA adapter weights are handled:

---

### 1. **LoRA A and B Matrix Storage/Access**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257)

The `get_lora_parameters()` function is the primary accessor for LoRA weights:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices via active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dict-based access
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                   # Extract weights
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Key storage patterns:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - Linear layer containing LoRA A matrix
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - Linear layer containing LoRA B matrix
- Both accessed as dictionaries indexed by adapter name (default: "default")

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 489-515)

Example usage in fast forward pass:
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight      # Shape: (r, in_features)
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight      # Shape: (out_features, r)
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]           # Scalar scale factor
```

---

### 2. **LoRA Adapter Data Structures**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA configuration is defined via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,                      # Rank (default 16)
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,            # Alpha scaling (default 16)
    target_modules      = final_modules,         # Which layers to adapt
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,          # Dropout rate (default 0.0)
    bias                = bias,                  # "none", "all", or "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,   # Task type enum
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,   # Which layers to patch
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,    # How to init ("gaussian", "loftq", True/False)
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,         # LoftQ quantization config
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,           # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,      # Non-LoRA trainable modules
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

**Key metadata stored in peft_config:**
- Located at `model.peft_config["default"]` after `get_peft_model()`
- Contains all hyperparameters for reconstruction
- Accessible via `.to_dict()` for inspection (line 2334)

**Target modules** (default for LLaMA):
- `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`

---

### 3. **Adapter Saving Format**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/save.py` (lines 205-450)

**Primary saving function:** `unsloth_save_model()`

When `save_method="lora"`:
```python
save_pretrained_settings["selected_adapters"] = None  # Save all adapters
model.save_pretrained(**save_pretrained_settings)     # Uses PEFT's save
```

**What gets saved:**
- **adapter_config.json** - LoRA configuration (rank, alpha, target_modules, etc.)
- **adapter_model.bin** - State dict containing:
  - `lora_A.*` weights (all LoRA A matrices)
  - `lora_B.*` weights (all LoRA B matrices)
  - Naming pattern: `base_model.model.layers.{i}.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight`
- **README.md** - Metadata for HuggingFace Hub

**Format:** PyTorch safetensors or pickle (configurable via `safe_serialization` parameter)

---

### 4. **Existing Methods for Iterating LoRA Layers/Weights**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/tests/utils/data_utils.py` (lines 132-142)

```python
def get_peft_weights(model):
    """Extract all LoRA weights from named_parameters()"""
    is_lora_weight = lambda name: any(s in name for s in ["lora_A", "lora_B"])
    return {
        name: param for name, param in model.named_parameters() 
        if is_lora_weight(name)
    }

def describe_peft_weights(model):
    """Iterate over LoRA weights with details"""
    for name, param in get_peft_weights(model).items():
        yield name, describe_param(param, as_str=True)
```

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2807-2895)

Patch function iterates through layers:
```python
for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
    # Check for LoRA adapters
    if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A"):  # Existence check
        mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
```

---

### 5. **get_peft_model and Adapter Patching**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2266-2661)

**Main flow:**
```python
@staticmethod
def get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=[...], lora_alpha=16, ...):
    # 1. Create LoraConfig
    lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
    
    # 2. Apply PEFT adapter injection (from peft import get_peft_model as _get_peft_model)
    model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
    
    # 3. Fix LoraConfig metadata
    fix_lora_auto_mapping(model)
    
    # 4. Apply QAT if specified
    if qat_scheme is not None:
        model = _prepare_model_for_qat(model, qat_scheme)
    
    # 5. Patch model with optimized LoRA kernels
    model = FastLlamaModel.patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing)
```

**PEFT integration:**
```python
from peft import LoraConfig, TaskType, get_peft_model as _get_peft_model
from peft import PeftModelForCausalLM, PeftModelForSequenceClassification
```

**Adapter patching** (line 2732-2947):
```python
@staticmethod
def patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth"):
    # Patches forward functions with fast LoRA implementations
    for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
        gate_proj = mlp_module.gate_proj
        
        if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A"):  # Check if LoRA present
            mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
            n_mlp += 1
        
        if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A"):
            layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
            n_qkv += 1
```

---

### 6. **Adapter Merging (LoRA + Base Weight Fusion)**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-187)

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merge LoRA adapters into base weights"""
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    # Dequantize if 4-bit
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge: W' = W + (s * A.T @ B.T)
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

---

### 7. **Fast LoRA Forward Computation**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 28-174)

Custom autograd functions for efficient computation:

```python
class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS, ...):
        # Q = X @ (W + (QA @ QB) * scale)
        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
        
    @staticmethod
    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
        # Compute gradients for A, B matrices separately
        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha=QS, beta=0)
        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha=QS, beta=0)
        # ... similar for K, V
```

---

| Weight Iteration | `/tests/utils/data_utils.py:132-142` | `get_peft_weights()`, `describe_peft_weights()` |

### **Key Data Structure Insights**

1. **Adapter access pattern:** Dictionary-based with adapter names as keys (typically "default")
2. **Weight shapes:**
   - `lora_A`: `(rank, input_dim)`
   - `lora_B`: `(output_dim, rank)`

## Unsloth LoRA Adapter Weights Handling - Complete Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how LoRA adapter weights are handled:

---

### 1. **LoRA A and B Matrix Storage/Access**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257)

The `get_lora_parameters()` function is the primary accessor for LoRA weights:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices via active adapter
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dict-based access
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                   # Extract weights
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Key storage patterns:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name]` - Linear layer containing LoRA A matrix
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name]` - Linear layer containing LoRA B matrix
- Both accessed as dictionaries indexed by adapter name (default: "default")

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 489-515)

Example usage in fast forward pass:
```python
lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight      # Shape: (r, in_features)
lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight      # Shape: (out_features, r)
scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]           # Scalar scale factor
```

---

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA configuration is defined via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,                      # Rank (default 16)
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,            # Alpha scaling (default 16)
    target_modules      = final_modules,         # Which layers to adapt
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,          # Dropout rate (default 0.0)
    bias                = bias,                  # "none", "all", or "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,   # Task type enum
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,   # Which layers to patch
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,    # How to init ("gaussian", "loftq", True/False)
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,         # LoftQ quantization config
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,           # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,      # Non-LoRA trainable modules
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
```

**Key metadata stored in peft_config:**
- Located at `model.peft_config["default"]` after `get_peft_model()`
- Contains all hyperparameters for reconstruction
- Accessible via `.to_dict()` for inspection (line 2334)

**Target modules** (default for LLaMA):

### 3. **Adapter Saving Format**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329274062-hsj60t/unsloth/save.py` (lines 205-450)

When `save_method="lora"`:

```python
save_pretrained_settings["selected_adapters"] = None  # Save all adapters

```

**What gets saved:**
- **adapter_config.json** - LoRA configuration (rank, alpha, target_modules, etc.)
- **adapter_model.bin** - State dict containing:
  - `lora_A.*` weights (all LoRA A matrices)
  - `lora_B.*` weights (all LoRA B matrices)
  - Naming pattern: `base_model.model.layers.{i}.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight`
- **README.md** - Metadata for HuggingFace Hub

**Format:** PyTorch safetensors or pickle (configurable via `safe_serialization` parameter)

```python
def get_peft_weights(model):
    """Extract all LoRA weights from named_parameters()"""
    is_lora_weight = lambda name: any(s in name for s in ["lora_A", "lora_B"])
    return {
        name: param for name, param in model.named_parameters() 
        if is_lora_weight(name)
    }

def describe_peft_weights(model):

    for name, param in get_peft_weights(model).items():

```

```python
def get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=[...], lora_alpha=16, ...):
```

```python
@staticmethod
def patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth"):
    # Patches forward functions with fast LoRA implementations
    for idx, layer in enumerate(model.model.model.layers):
        gate_proj = mlp_module.gate_proj
        
        if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A"):  # Check if LoRA present
            mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
            n_mlp += 1

            layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
            n_qkv += 1
```

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the unsloth codebase. Let me create the final report:

## Unsloth Codebase Architecture Report

### 1. Top-Level Directory Layout

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point, exports public API
├── save.py                     # Main save/export module (2678 lines)
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utility functions
├── trainer.py                  # Training utilities
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── models/                     # Model implementations
│   ├── __init__.py            # Exports FastLanguageModel, FastModel, etc.
│   ├── loader.py              # Main loader for models (from_pretrained)
│   ├── loader_utils.py        # Loader utilities
│   ├── llama.py               # Llama model optimizations
│   ├── mistral.py             # Mistral model optimizations
│   ├── qwen2.py               # Qwen2 model optimizations
│   ├── qwen3.py               # Qwen3 model optimizations
│   ├── vision.py              # Vision model support
│   ├── rl.py                  # RL/DPO model support
│   ├── _utils.py              # Model utility functions
│   └── [other model files]
├── kernels/                    # CUDA/optimization kernels
├── registry/                   # Model registry
├── dataprep/                   # Data preparation utilities
└── utils/                      # General utilities
```

### 2. Files Handling Model Saving, Exporting, and Adapter Management

**Primary File: `/unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)**
- Central module for all save/export functionality
- Imports from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` and `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp` for core merge/GGUF operations

### 3. GGUF Export Implementation

Located in `/unsloth/save.py`:

- **`save_to_gguf()` (line 952)**
  - Main function for GGUF conversion
  - Signature: `save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece, model_directory, quantization_method, first_conversion, _run_installer)`
  - Handles llama.cpp installation and quantization
  - Supports multiple quantization methods: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `bf16`, etc.

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768)** - Instance method
  - Converts 4bit → float16 → GGUF format
  - Signature includes: `save_directory`, `tokenizer`, `quantization_method`, `first_conversion`, `push_to_hub`, `token`
  - Attached to models via `patch_saving_functions()`

- **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (line 1951)** - Instance method
  - Pushes GGUF models to Hugging Face Hub
  - Similar signature to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` but with `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`

- **`save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276)**
  - Generic GGUF export for non-Llama models
  - Supports optional Hub upload via `repo_id`

### 4. Adapter Saving Implementation

- **`unsloth_save_model()` (line 205)** - Core function
  - Main save function handling three modes:
    - `"lora"` - Save only LoRA adapters (no merging)
    - `"merged_16bit"` - Merge LoRA into float16 weights
    - `"merged_4bit"` - Merge LoRA into int4 weights
  - Signature: `unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, push_to_hub, token, ...)`
  - Handles adapter selection via PEFT interface
  - Calls `model.save_pretrained()` internally for actual saving

- **`save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2129)**
  - Helper function to save only LoRA adapters to custom directory

- **LoRA Conversion Functions:**
  - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211)
  - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (line 2143)

### 5. Merged Model Saving Implementation

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321)** - Instance method
  - Wrapper around `unsloth_save_model()` with auto-merge capability
  - Parameters: `save_directory`, `tokenizer`, `save_method`, `push_to_hub`
  - Attached to models via `patch_saving_functions()`

- **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 1364)** - Instance method
  - Merges and pushes to Hub in one operation
  - Parameters: `repo_id`, `tokenizer`, `save_method`, `push_to_hub`, `token`

- **Generic versions:**
  - `unsloth_generic_save()` (line 2356)

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the unsloth codebase. Let me create the final report:

## Unsloth Codebase Architecture Report

### 1. Top-Level Directory Layout

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point, exports public API
├── save.py                     # Main save/export module (2678 lines)
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utility functions
├── trainer.py                  # Training utilities
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── models/                     # Model implementations
│   ├── __init__.py            # Exports FastLanguageModel, FastModel, etc.
│   ├── loader.py              # Main loader for models (from_pretrained)
│   ├── loader_utils.py        # Loader utilities
│   ├── llama.py               # Llama model optimizations
│   ├── mistral.py             # Mistral model optimizations
│   ├── qwen2.py               # Qwen2 model optimizations
│   ├── qwen3.py               # Qwen3 model optimizations
│   ├── vision.py              # Vision model support
│   ├── rl.py                  # RL/DPO model support
│   ├── _utils.py              # Model utility functions
│   └── [other model files]
├── kernels/                    # CUDA/optimization kernels
├── registry/                   # Model registry
├── dataprep/                   # Data preparation utilities
└── utils/                      # General utilities
```

### 2. Files Handling Model Saving, Exporting, and Adapter Management

**Primary File: `/unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)**
- Central module for all save/export functionality
- Imports from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` and `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp` for core merge/GGUF operations

### 3. GGUF Export Implementation

Located in `/unsloth/save.py`:

- **`save_to_gguf()` (line 952)**
  - Main function for GGUF conversion
  - Signature: `save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece, model_directory, quantization_method, first_conversion, _run_installer)`
  - Handles llama.cpp installation and quantization
  - Supports multiple quantization methods: `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q8_0`, `f16`, `bf16`, etc.

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768)** - Instance method
  - Converts 4bit → float16 → GGUF format
  - Signature includes: `save_directory`, `tokenizer`, `quantization_method`, `first_conversion`, `push_to_hub`, `token`
  - Attached to models via `patch_saving_functions()`

- **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (line 1951)** - Instance method
  - Pushes GGUF models to Hugging Face Hub
  - Similar signature to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` but with `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`

- **`save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276)**
  - Generic GGUF export for non-Llama models
  - Supports optional Hub upload via `repo_id`

### 4. Adapter Saving Implementation

- **`unsloth_save_model()` (line 205)** - Core function
  - Main save function handling three modes:
    - `"lora"` - Save only LoRA adapters (no merging)
    - `"merged_16bit"` - Merge LoRA into float16 weights
    - `"merged_4bit"` - Merge LoRA into int4 weights
  - Signature: `unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method, push_to_hub, token, ...)`
  - Handles adapter selection via PEFT interface
  - Calls `model.save_pretrained()` internally for actual saving

- **`save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2129)**
  - Helper function to save only LoRA adapters to custom directory

- **LoRA Conversion Functions:**
  - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211)
  - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (line 2143)

### 5. Merged Model Saving Implementation

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321)** - Instance method
  - Wrapper around `unsloth_save_model()` with auto-merge capability
  - Parameters: `save_directory`, `tokenizer`, `save_method`, `push_to_hub`
  - Attached to models via `patch_saving_functions()`

- **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 1364)** - Instance method
  - Merges and pushes to Hub in one operation
  - Parameters: `repo_id`, `tokenizer`, `save_method`, `push_to_hub`, `token`

- **Generic versions:**
  - `unsloth_generic_save()` (line 2356)
  - `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 2413)
  - `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 2456)

### 6. TorchAO Quantization

- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502)** - Instance method
  - Merges LoRA then quantizes with TorchAO
  - Saves quantized checkpoint locally or to Hub
  - Signature: `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config, push_to_hub, token)`

### 7. How Existing Save/Export Methods Are Structured

**Naming Convention:**
- Instance methods: `unsloth_save_pretrained_*()` and `unsloth_push_to_hub_*()`
- Module functions: `unsloth_save_model()`, `save_to_gguf()`, `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`
- Patching function: `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`

**Patching Mechanism (line 2569):**
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Attaches these methods to model instance:
    model.save_pretrained_merged()
    model.push_to_hub_merged()
    model.save_pretrained_gguf()
    model.push_to_hub_gguf()
    model.save_pretrained_torchao()
    model.push_to_hub_ggml()
    model.save_pretrained_ggml()
```

### 8. ONNX-Related Code

**Status: No ONNX support currently implemented**
- No files found containing "onnx" or "ONNX"
- GGUF is the primary export format for quantized/merged models

### 9. Main Module Entry Points

**`/unsloth/__init__.py`:**
```python
from .models import *  # Exports FastLanguageModel, FastModel, etc.
from .save import *    # Exports save/export functions
```

**Public API exports from `save.py` (__all__):**
```python
[
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

### 10. Key Dependencies

- **PEFT**: Used for LoRA adapter management (`peft.tuners.lora`)
- **unsloth_zoo**: Provides:
  - `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` - Core merge logic
  - `prepare_saving()` - HF repo preparation
  - `convert_to_gguf()` - GGUF conversion logic
  - `install_llama_cpp()` - llama.cpp setup
- **transformers**: Model saving/loading infrastructure
- **huggingface_hub**: Hub integration for push operations

### 11. Save Method Signature Patterns

**All main save functions follow this pattern:**
```
save_directory/repo_id: str or PathLike
tokenizer: Optional (warning if not provided for some formats)
save_method: str ("lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit")
quantization_method: str (for GGUF: "q4_k_m", "q8_0", etc.)
push_to_hub: bool
token: Optional[str]
is_main_process: bool
state_dict: Optional[dict]
max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB"
safe_serialization: bool = True
temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers"
maximum_memory_usage: float (varies by method)
```

### 12. File Paths Summary

| Functionality | File Path | Key Functions |
|---------------|-----------|---|
| Main Save API | `/unsloth/save.py` | `unsloth_save_model()`, `patch_saving_functions()` |
| GGUF Export | `/unsloth/save.py` | `save_to_gguf()`, `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` |
| Merged Model | `/unsloth/save.py` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`, `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` |
| Adapter Save | `/unsloth/save.py` | `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`, adapter handling in `unsloth_save_model()` |
| Model Loading | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`, `FastModel.from_pretrained()` |
| LoRA Config | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | `get_peft_model()` method (line 2266) |
| TorchAO Quantization | `/unsloth/save.py` | `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` |

gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

        link = f"{username}/{new_save_directory.lstrip('/.')}" \
            if username not in new_save_directory else \
            new_save_directory.lstrip('/.')

        print(f"Saved GGUF to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    pass

    # Save modelfile
    if modelfile_location is not None:
        username = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, repo_id, token,
            "GGUF converted", "gguf", modelfile_location, old_username, private,
        )
        print(f"Saved Ollama Modelfile to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    pass

    if fix_bos_token:

        logger.warning(
            "Unsloth: ##### The current model auto adds a BOS token.\n"\
            "Unsloth: ##### We removed it in GGUF's chat template for you."
        )
    pass
pass


# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return
pass


def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
