import warnings, importlib, sys
from packaging.version import Version
import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
import numpy as np

# Fix some issues before importing other packages
from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;

# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]

# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.

if already_imported:
    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
    warnings.warn(
        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
pass

# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!

# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"

# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work
#    "pinned_use_cuda_host_register:True,"\
#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"

# Log Unsloth is being used
os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"

# Try importing PyTorch and check version
try:
    import torch
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

# Fix other issues
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue
fix_vllm_aimv2_issue(); del fix_vllm_aimv2_issue;
from .import_fixes import ignore_logger_messages
ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
pass

# For Gradio HF Spaces?
# if "SPACE_AUTHOR_NAME" not in os.environ and "SPACE_REPO_NAME" not in os.environ:
import triton
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
        except: pass
    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs

    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
    import bitsandbytes as bnb
    try:
        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
        libcuda_dirs()
    except:
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
        )

        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
        pass

        importlib.reload(bnb)
        importlib.reload(triton)
        try:
            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
                except: pass
            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
            libcuda_dirs()
        except:
            warnings.warn(
                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
            )
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    # NO-OP for rocm device
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
    pass

# Check for unsloth_zoo
try:
    unsloth_zoo_version = importlib_version("unsloth_zoo")
    if Version(unsloth_zoo_version) < Version("2025.9.6"):
        print(
            "Unsloth: Please update Unsloth and Unsloth-Zoo to the latest version!\n"\
            "Do this via `pip install --upgrade --force-reinstall --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth unsloth_zoo`"
        )
        # if os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES", "0") == "0":
        #     try:
        #         os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo")
        #     except:
        #         try:
        #             os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps --user unsloth_zoo")
        #         except:
        #             raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please update unsloth_zoo via `pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo`")
    import unsloth_zoo
except:
    raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please install unsloth_zoo via `pip install unsloth_zoo`")
pass

from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()

input.content:
# Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters in Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth supports several export formats for trained LoRA models (GGUF, GGML, merged fp16, TorchAO), but has no ONNX export path. ONNX is a widely-used interchange format for ML runtimes (ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, TensorRT, etc.). The goal is to add `export_lora_to_onnx` that serialises the raw LoRA adapter weight tensors (A and B matrices) into a valid ONNX model file along with a JSON metadata sidecar, following the same code patterns already established in `save.py`.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | **Only file modified.** All save/export logic lives here. |
| `unsloth/__init__.py` | Re-exports `from .save import *` — no changes needed; `__all__` in `save.py` controls public API. |
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Provides `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (already imported in `save.py`). Read-only reference. |

---

## Key Existing Patterns to Reuse

- **Module detection** (`save.py:162`): `isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` — already imported at top of file.
- **LoRA weight access** (`kernels/utils.py:214–231`): `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (shape `(rank, in_features)`) and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (shape `(out_features, rank)`); active adapter via `getattr(proj, "active_adapters", …)`.
- **LoRA metadata** (`models/llama.py:2598`): Lives in `model.peft_config["default"]` — a `peft.LoraConfig` with `.r`, `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`.
- **Instance-method wrapper pattern** (`save.py:2502`): Standalone function with `self` as first arg, attached to the model object in `patch_saving_functions()` via `types.MethodType`.
- **Lazy optional import**: heavy optional dependencies are imported inside the function body (see `from torchao import quantize_` at line 2530).
- **`@torch.inference_mode` decorator**: applied to all export functions that read weights (`unsloth_save_model`, `unsloth_generic_save`, etc.).

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Update `__all__` in `save.py` (line 48–54)

Add `"export_lora_to_onnx"` to the existing list:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_lora_to_onnx",          # ← add this
]
```

---

### Step 2 — Add `export_lora_to_onnx()` module-level function (insert after line 2141, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

Location anchor: right after the closing of `save_lora_to_custom_dir` (the blank line at ~line 2141).

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def export_lora_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Export LoRA adapter weights from an Unsloth / PEFT model to ONNX format.

    The exported ONNX file is a *weight container*: it has no computation
    graph nodes, only ONNX initializers (named tensors) — one ``lora_A``
    and one ``lora_B`` entry per targeted module.  A JSON sidecar file
    (``lora_metadata.json``) is written alongside the ``.onnx`` file and
    contains the adapter hyper-parameters.

    Args:
        model: A PEFT-wrapped unsloth model (returned by
            ``FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model`` or equivalent).
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
            Created automatically if it does not exist.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Raises:
        ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
        ValueError: If no trainable LoRA adapter layers are found in *model*.
    """
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import numpy_helper, helper
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it with: pip install onnx"
        )
    import json

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Read LoRA hyper-parameters from peft_config ────────────────────
    rank           = None
    lora_alpha     = None
    target_modules = None

    if hasattr(model, "peft_config") and model.peft_config:
        adapter_name   = next(iter(model.peft_config))   # typically "default"
        peft_cfg       = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
        rank           = getattr(peft_cfg, "r",              None)
        lora_alpha     = getattr(peft_cfg, "lora_alpha",     None)
        target_modules = getattr(peft_cfg, "target_modules", None)
    pass

    # ── 2. Iterate all modules and collect LoRA A / B weights ─────────────
    initializers   = []   # ONNX TensorProto list
    layer_metadata = {}   # per-layer info for JSON sidecar

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        # Skip merged or disabled adapters (they have no separate A/B)
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", True) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        active = active[0]

        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and active in module.lora_A):
            continue

        A_weight = module.lora_A[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        B_weight = module.lora_B[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        scale    = float(module.scaling[active])

        a_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_A.weight"
        b_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_B.weight"

        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(A_weight, name=a_tensor_name))
        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(B_weight, name=b_tensor_name))

        layer_metadata[module_name] = {
            "lora_A_shape" : list(A_weight.shape),   # (rank, in_features)
            "lora_B_shape" : list(B_weight.shape),   # (out_features, rank)
            "scale"        : scale,
        }
    pass

    if not initializers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found in the model. "
            "Make sure the model was created with `get_peft_model()` and the "
            "adapters have not been merged."
        )
    pass

    # ── 3. Build minimal ONNX graph (no nodes — weight-container only) ────
    graph = helper.make_graph(
        nodes       = [],
        name        = "lora_adapters",
        inputs      = [],
        outputs     = [],
        initializer = initializers,
    )

    model_proto = helper.make_model(
        graph,
        opset_imports = [helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        ir_version    = 8,   # compatible with opset 17
    )
    model_proto.doc_string = (
        "LoRA adapter weights exported by Unsloth. "
        "Each initializer is a lora_A or lora_B weight matrix. "
        "See lora_metadata.json for hyper-parameters."
    )

    # ── 4. Embed metadata as ONNX model properties ────────────────────────
    meta_dict = {
        "lora_rank"      : str(rank),
        "lora_alpha"     : str(lora_alpha),
        "target_modules" : str(target_modules),
        "opset_version"  : str(opset_version),
        "framework"      : "unsloth",
    }
    for key, value in meta_dict.items():
        entry       = model_proto.metadata_props.add()
        entry.key   = key
        entry.value = value
    pass

    # ── 5. Save ONNX file ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapters.onnx")
    onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA ONNX weights to {onnx_path}")

    # ── 6. Save JSON metadata sidecar ─────────────────────────────────────
    full_metadata = {
        "lora_rank"      : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : list(target_modules) if target_modules else None,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "layers"         : layer_metadata,
    }
    json_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(json_path, "w") as fh:
        json.dump(full_metadata, fh, indent=2)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA metadata to {json_path}")

    return onnx_path
pass
```

---

### Step 3 — Add `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx()` instance-method wrapper (insert after `export_lora_to_onnx`, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

This thin wrapper mirrors the pattern of every other `unsloth_save_pretrained_*` function (compare `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` at line 2502).

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Save LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    Wraps :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` as an instance method attached to the
    model by :func:`patch_saving_functions`.

    Args:
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Example::

        model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
        model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, ...)
        # ... fine-tune ...
        model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_lora_onnx/")
    """
    return export_lora_to_onnx(
        model         = self,
        output_dir    = output_dir,
        opset_version = opset_version,
    )
pass
```

---

### Step 4 — Register method in `patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2657–2676)

In the `if not vision:` branch (around line 2664) and the `else:` vision branch (around line 2671), add one line each:

**Non-vision branch** (after line 2667, inside the `if hasattr(model, "config"):` block):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
```

**Vision branch** (after line 2675):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
```

The resulting block looks like:

```python
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
            model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)  # ← new
    else:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
        model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,           model)  # ← new
```

---

## Design Decisions & Rationale

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Weight-container ONNX graph** (initializers, no nodes) | LoRA adapters are pure weight deltas, not standalone runnable graphs. This is the standard way to package tensors in ONNX without inventing a dummy inference graph. |
| **Float32 conversion** | ONNX doesn't natively represent quantized 4-bit weights; converting to fp32 ensures universal compatibility with ONNX Runtime and downstream tools. |
| **Lazy `import onnx`** | `onnx` is an optional dependency; failing at call-time (not import-time) is consistent with other optional deps in the file (torchao, huggingface_hub). |
| **JSON sidecar** | ONNX metadata_props stores only strings; a separate JSON file makes hyper-params easily accessible by downstream code without parsing ONNX. |
| **Single `.onnx` file** | All adapter layers in one file is simpler to manage than one file per layer. Layer names are embedded in tensor names. |
| **Both vision and non-vision branches** | `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` doesn't depend on model architecture; registering it for vision models is harmless and consistent. |

---

## Verification

After implementation, verify end-to-end (without running unsloth itself, as instructed):

1. **Static import check**: Confirm `export_lora_to_onnx` appears in `__all__` in `save.py`.
2. **Function presence**: Grep for `def export_lora_to_onnx` and `def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` in `save.py`.
3. **Registration check**: Grep for `save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions` body.
4. **Syntax check**: `python -m py_compile unsloth/save.py` (no unsloth runtime needed).
5. **Unit test (when runnable)**: Create a tiny mock PEFT model, call `export_lora_to_onnx(model, "/tmp/test_onnx")`, assert `lora_adapters.onnx` and `lora_metadata.json` are created, load the ONNX model with `onnx.load()` and `onnx.checker.check_model()`, verify initializer names and metadata_props.

# Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters in Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth supports several export formats for trained LoRA models (GGUF, GGML, merged fp16, TorchAO), but has no ONNX export path. ONNX is a widely-used interchange format for ML runtimes (ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, TensorRT, etc.). The goal is to add `export_lora_to_onnx` that serialises the raw LoRA adapter weight tensors (A and B matrices) into a valid ONNX model file along with a JSON metadata sidecar, following the same code patterns already established in `save.py`.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | **Only file modified.** All save/export logic lives here. |
| `unsloth/__init__.py` | Re-exports `from .save import *` — no changes needed; `__all__` in `save.py` controls public API. |
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Provides `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (already imported in `save.py`). Read-only reference. |

---

## Key Existing Patterns to Reuse

- **Module detection** (`save.py:162`): `isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` — already imported at top of file.
- **LoRA weight access** (`kernels/utils.py:214–231`): `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (shape `(rank, in_features)`) and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (shape `(out_features, rank)`); active adapter via `getattr(proj, "active_adapters", …)`.
- **LoRA metadata** (`models/llama.py:2598`): Lives in `model.peft_config["default"]` — a `peft.LoraConfig` with `.r`, `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`.
- **Instance-method wrapper pattern** (`save.py:2502`): Standalone function with `self` as first arg, attached to the model object in `patch_saving_functions()` via `types.MethodType`.
- **Lazy optional import**: heavy optional dependencies are imported inside the function body (see `from torchao import quantize_` at line 2530).
- **`@torch.inference_mode` decorator**: applied to all export functions that read weights (`unsloth_save_model`, `unsloth_generic_save`, etc.).

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Update `__all__` in `save.py` (line 48–54)

Add `"export_lora_to_onnx"` to the existing list:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_lora_to_onnx",          # ← add this
]
```

---

### Step 2 — Add `export_lora_to_onnx()` module-level function (insert after line 2141, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

Location anchor: right after the closing of `save_lora_to_custom_dir` (the blank line at ~line 2141).

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def export_lora_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Export LoRA adapter weights from an Unsloth / PEFT model to ONNX format.

    The exported ONNX file is a *weight container*: it has no computation
    graph nodes, only ONNX initializers (named tensors) — one ``lora_A``
    and one ``lora_B`` entry per targeted module.  A JSON sidecar file
    (``lora_metadata.json``) is written alongside the ``.onnx`` file and
    contains the adapter hyper-parameters.

    Args:
        model: A PEFT-wrapped unsloth model (returned by
            ``FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model`` or equivalent).
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
            Created automatically if it does not exist.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Raises:
        ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
        ValueError: If no trainable LoRA adapter layers are found in *model*.
    """
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import numpy_helper, helper
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it with: pip install onnx"
        )
    import json

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Read LoRA hyper-parameters from peft_config ────────────────────
    rank           = None
    lora_alpha     = None
    target_modules = None

    if hasattr(model, "peft_config") and model.peft_config:
        adapter_name   = next(iter(model.peft_config))   # typically "default"
        peft_cfg       = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
        rank           = getattr(peft_cfg, "r",              None)
        lora_alpha     = getattr(peft_cfg, "lora_alpha",     None)
        target_modules = getattr(peft_cfg, "target_modules", None)
    pass

    # ── 2. Iterate all modules and collect LoRA A / B weights ─────────────
    initializers   = []   # ONNX TensorProto list
    layer_metadata = {}   # per-layer info for JSON sidecar

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        # Skip merged or disabled adapters (they have no separate A/B)
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", True) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        active = active[0]

        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and active in module.lora_A):
            continue

        A_weight = module.lora_A[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        B_weight = module.lora_B[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        scale    = float(module.scaling[active])

        a_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_A.weight"
        b_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_B.weight"

        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(A_weight, name=a_tensor_name))
        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(B_weight, name=b_tensor_name))

        layer_metadata[module_name] = {
            "lora_A_shape" : list(A_weight.shape),   # (rank, in_features)
            "lora_B_shape" : list(B_weight.shape),   # (out_features, rank)
            "scale"        : scale,
        }
    pass

    if not initializers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found in the model. "
            "Make sure the model was created with `get_peft_model()` and the "
            "adapters have not been merged."
        )
    pass

    # ── 3. Build minimal ONNX graph (no nodes — weight-container only) ────
    graph = helper.make_graph(
        nodes       = [],
        name        = "lora_adapters",
        inputs      = [],
        outputs     = [],
        initializer = initializers,
    )

    model_proto = helper.make_model(
        graph,
        opset_imports = [helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        ir_version    = 8,   # compatible with opset 17
    )
    model_proto.doc_string = (
        "LoRA adapter weights exported by Unsloth. "
        "Each initializer is a lora_A or lora_B weight matrix. "
        "See lora_metadata.json for hyper-parameters."
    )

    # ── 4. Embed metadata as ONNX model properties ────────────────────────
    meta_dict = {
        "lora_rank"      : str(rank),
        "lora_alpha"     : str(lora_alpha),
        "target_modules" : str(target_modules),
        "opset_version"  : str(opset_version),
        "framework"      : "unsloth",
    }
    for key, value in meta_dict.items():
        entry       = model_proto.metadata_props.add()
        entry.key   = key
        entry.value = value
    pass

    # ── 5. Save ONNX file ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapters.onnx")
    onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA ONNX weights to {onnx_path}")

    # ── 6. Save JSON metadata sidecar ─────────────────────────────────────
    full_metadata = {
        "lora_rank"      : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : list(target_modules) if target_modules else None,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "layers"         : layer_metadata,
    }
    json_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(json_path, "w") as fh:
        json.dump(full_metadata, fh, indent=2)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA metadata to {json_path}")

    return onnx_path
pass
```

---

### Step 3 — Add `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx()` instance-method wrapper (insert after `export_lora_to_onnx`, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

This thin wrapper mirrors the pattern of every other `unsloth_save_pretrained_*` function (compare `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` at line 2502).

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Save LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    Wraps :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` as an instance method attached to the
    model by :func:`patch_saving_functions`.

    Args:
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Example::

        model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
        model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, ...)
        # ... fine-tune ...
        model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_lora_onnx/")
    """
    return export_lora_to_onnx(
        model         = self,
        output_dir    = output_dir,
        opset_version = opset_version,
    )
pass
```

---

### Step 4 — Register method in `patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2657–2676)

In the `if not vision:` branch (around line 2664) and the `else:` vision branch (around line 2671), add one line each:

**Non-vision branch** (after line 2667, inside the `if hasattr(model, "config"):` block):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
```

**Vision branch** (after line 2675):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
```

The resulting block looks like:

```python
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
            model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)  # ← new
    else:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
        model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,           model)  # ← new
```

---

## Design Decisions & Rationale

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Weight-container ONNX graph** (initializers, no nodes) | LoRA adapters are pure weight deltas, not standalone runnable graphs. This is the standard way to package tensors in ONNX without inventing a dummy inference graph. |

# Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters in Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth supports several export formats for trained LoRA models (GGUF, GGML, merged fp16, TorchAO), but has no ONNX export path. ONNX is a widely-used interchange format for ML runtimes (ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, TensorRT, etc.). The goal is to add `export_lora_to_onnx` that serialises the raw LoRA adapter weight tensors (A and B matrices) into a valid ONNX model file along with a JSON metadata sidecar, following the same code patterns already established in `save.py`.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | **Only file modified.** All save/export logic lives here. |
| `unsloth/__init__.py` | Re-exports `from .save import *` — no changes needed; `__all__` in `save.py` controls public API. |
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Provides `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (already imported in `save.py`). Read-only reference. |

---

## Key Existing Patterns to Reuse

- **Module detection** (`save.py:162`): `isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` — already imported at top of file.
- **LoRA weight access** (`kernels/utils.py:214–231`): `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (shape `(rank, in_features)`) and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (shape `(out_features, rank)`); active adapter via `getattr(proj, "active_adapters", …)`.
- **LoRA metadata** (`models/llama.py:2598`): Lives in `model.peft_config["default"]` — a `peft.LoraConfig` with `.r`, `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`.
- **Instance-method wrapper pattern** (`save.py:2502`): Standalone function with `self` as first arg, attached to the model object in `patch_saving_functions()` via `types.MethodType`.
- **Lazy optional import**: heavy optional dependencies are imported inside the function body (see `from torchao import quantize_` at line 2530).
- **`@torch.inference_mode` decorator**: applied to all export functions that read weights (`unsloth_save_model`, `unsloth_generic_save`, etc.).

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Update `__all__` in `save.py` (line 48–54)

Add `"export_lora_to_onnx"` to the existing list:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_lora_to_onnx",          # ← add this
]
```

---

### Step 2 — Add `export_lora_to_onnx()` module-level function (insert after line 2141, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

Location anchor: right after the closing of `save_lora_to_custom_dir` (the blank line at ~line 2141).

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def export_lora_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Export LoRA adapter weights from an Unsloth / PEFT model to ONNX format.

    The exported ONNX file is a *weight container*: it has no computation
    graph nodes, only ONNX initializers (named tensors) — one ``lora_A``
    and one ``lora_B`` entry per targeted module.  A JSON sidecar file
    (``lora_metadata.json``) is written alongside the ``.onnx`` file and
    contains the adapter hyper-parameters.

    Args:
        model: A PEFT-wrapped unsloth model (returned by
            ``FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model`` or equivalent).
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
            Created automatically if it does not exist.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Raises:
        ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
        ValueError: If no trainable LoRA adapter layers are found in *model*.
    """
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import numpy_helper, helper
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it with: pip install onnx"
        )
    import json

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Read LoRA hyper-parameters from peft_config ────────────────────
    rank           = None
    lora_alpha     = None
    target_modules = None

    if hasattr(model, "peft_config") and model.peft_config:
        adapter_name   = next(iter(model.peft_config))   # typically "default"
        peft_cfg       = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
        rank           = getattr(peft_cfg, "r",              None)
        lora_alpha     = getattr(peft_cfg, "lora_alpha",     None)
        target_modules = getattr(peft_cfg, "target_modules", None)
    pass

    # ── 2. Iterate all modules and collect LoRA A / B weights ─────────────
    initializers   = []   # ONNX TensorProto list
    layer_metadata = {}   # per-layer info for JSON sidecar

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        # Skip merged or disabled adapters (they have no separate A/B)
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", True) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        active = active[0]

        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and active in module.lora_A):
            continue

        A_weight = module.lora_A[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        B_weight = module.lora_B[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        scale    = float(module.scaling[active])

        a_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_A.weight"
        b_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_B.weight"

        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(A_weight, name=a_tensor_name))
        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(B_weight, name=b_tensor_name))

        layer_metadata[module_name] = {
            "lora_A_shape" : list(A_weight.shape),   # (rank, in_features)
            "lora_B_shape" : list(B_weight.shape),   # (out_features, rank)
            "scale"        : scale,
        }
    pass

    if not initializers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found in the model. "
            "Make sure the model was created with `get_peft_model()` and the "
            "adapters have not been merged."
        )
    pass

    # ── 3. Build minimal ONNX graph (no nodes — weight-container only) ────
    graph = helper.make_graph(
        nodes       = [],
        name        = "lora_adapters",
        inputs      = [],
        outputs     = [],
        initializer = initializers,
    )

    model_proto = helper.make_model(
        graph,
        opset_imports = [helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        ir_version    = 8,   # compatible with opset 17
    )
    model_proto.doc_string = (
        "LoRA adapter weights exported by Unsloth. "
        "Each initializer is a lora_A or lora_B weight matrix. "
        "See lora_metadata.json for hyper-parameters."
    )

    # ── 4. Embed metadata as ONNX model properties ────────────────────────
    meta_dict = {
        "lora_rank"      : str(rank),
        "lora_alpha"     : str(lora_alpha),
        "target_modules" : str(target_modules),
        "opset_version"  : str(opset_version),
        "framework"      : "unsloth",
    }
    for key, value in meta_dict.items():
        entry       = model_proto.metadata_props.add()
        entry.key   = key
        entry.value = value
    pass

    # ── 5. Save ONNX file ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapters.onnx")
    onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA ONNX weights to {onnx_path}")

    # ── 6. Save JSON metadata sidecar ─────────────────────────────────────
    full_metadata = {
        "lora_rank"      : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : list(target_modules) if target_modules else None,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "layers"         : layer_metadata,
    }
    json_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(json_path, "w") as fh:
        json.dump(full_metadata, fh, indent=2)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA metadata to {json_path}")

    return onnx_path
pass
```

---

### Step 3 — Add `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx()` instance-method wrapper (insert after `export_lora_to_onnx`, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

This thin wrapper mirrors the pattern of every other `unsloth_save_pretrained_*` function (compare `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` at line 2502).

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Save LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    Wraps :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` as an instance method attached to the
    model by :func:`patch_saving_functions`.

    Args:
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Example::

        model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
        model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, ...)
        # ... fine-tune ...
        model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_lora_onnx/")
    """
    return export_lora_to_onnx(
        model         = self,
        output_dir    = output_dir,
        opset_version = opset_version,
    )
pass
```

---

### Step 4 — Register method in `patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2657–2676)

In the `if not vision:` branch (around line 2664) and the `else:` vision branch (around line 2671), add one line each:

**Non-vision branch** (after line 2667, inside the `if hasattr(model, "config"):` block):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
```

**Vision branch** (after line 2675):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
```

The resulting block looks like:

```python
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
            model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,                  model)  # ← new
    else:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
        model.save_pretrained_onnx   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx,           model)  # ← new
```

---

## Design Decisions & Rationale

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Weight-container ONNX graph** (initializers, no nodes) | LoRA adapters are pure weight deltas, not standalone runnable graphs. This is the standard way to package tensors in ONNX without inventing a dummy inference graph. |
| **Float32 conversion** | ONNX doesn't natively represent quantized 4-bit weights; converting to fp32 ensures universal compatibility with ONNX Runtime and downstream tools. |
| **Lazy `import onnx`** | `onnx` is an optional dependency; failing at call-time (not import-time) is consistent with other optional deps in the file (torchao, huggingface_hub). |
| **JSON sidecar** | ONNX metadata_props stores only strings; a separate JSON file makes hyper-params easily accessible by downstream code without parsing ONNX. |
| **Single `.onnx` file** | All adapter layers in one file is simpler to manage than one file per layer. Layer names are embedded in tensor names. |
| **Both vision and non-vision branches** | `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` doesn't depend on model architecture; registering it for vision models is harmless and consistent. |

---

## Verification

After implementation, verify end-to-end (without running unsloth itself, as instructed):

1. **Static import check**: Confirm `export_lora_to_onnx` appears in `__all__` in `save.py`.
2. **Function presence**: Grep for `def export_lora_to_onnx` and `def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` in `save.py`.
3. **Registration check**: Grep for `save_pretrained_onnx` in `patch_saving_functions` body.
4. **Syntax check**: `python -m py_compile unsloth/save.py` (no unsloth runtime needed).
5. **Unit test (when runnable)**: Create a tiny mock PEFT model, call `export_lora_to_onnx(model, "/tmp/test_onnx")`, assert `lora_adapters.onnx` and `lora_metadata.json` are created, load the ONNX model with `onnx.load()` and `onnx.checker.check_model()`, verify initializer names and metadata_props.

---

## Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters

**Single file to modify: `unsloth/save.py`**

### 4 changes, in order:

**1. `__all__` (line 48–54)** — Add `"export_lora_to_onnx"` to the public API list.

- Reads `model.peft_config[adapter_name]` for rank / alpha / target_modules

- Accesses `module.lora_A[adapter].weight` (float32 cast) and `module.lora_B[adapter].weight` — same access pattern as `kernels/utils.py:get_lora_parameters()`

- Writes `lora_adapters.onnx` + `lora_metadata.json` to `output_dir`
- Raises `ValueError` if no LoRA layers found

**3. `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx()` wrapper** (right after the above):

- Same thin-wrapper pattern as `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` (line 2502)

**4. `patch_saving_functions()` registration** (lines 2658–2676):
- Add `model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)` in both the `if not vision:` and `else:` branches so all model types expose `model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_dir/")`

### Why a weight-container ONNX?
LoRA adapters are weight deltas, not runnable inference graphs. Storing them as ONNX initializers (named tensors, no ops) is the standard approach and keeps full compatibility with ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, and other consumers without inventing a dummy computation graph.

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: ONNX Export for LoRA Adapters in Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth supports several export formats for trained LoRA models (GGUF, GGML, merged fp16, TorchAO), but has no ONNX export path. ONNX is a widely-used interchange format for ML runtimes (ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, TensorRT, etc.). The goal is to add `export_lora_to_onnx` that serialises the raw LoRA adapter weight tensors (A and B matrices) into a valid ONNX model file along with a JSON metadata sidecar, following the same code patterns already established in `save.py`.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | **Only file modified.** All save/export logic lives here. |
| `unsloth/__init__.py` | Re-exports `from .save import *` — no changes needed; `__all__` in `save.py` controls public API. |
| `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Provides `get_lora_parameters_bias()` (already imported in `save.py`). Read-only reference. |

---

## Key Existing Patterns to Reuse

- **Module detection** (`save.py:162`): `isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear))` — already imported at top of file.
- **LoRA weight access** (`kernels/utils.py:214–231`): `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (shape `(rank, in_features)`) and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (shape `(out_features, rank)`); active adapter via `getattr(proj, "active_adapters", …)`.
- **LoRA metadata** (`models/llama.py:2598`): Lives in `model.peft_config["default"]` — a `peft.LoraConfig` with `.r`, `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`.
- **Instance-method wrapper pattern** (`save.py:2502`): Standalone function with `self` as first arg, attached to the model object in `patch_saving_functions()` via `types.MethodType`.
- **Lazy optional import**: heavy optional dependencies are imported inside the function body (see `from torchao import quantize_` at line 2530).
- **`@torch.inference_mode` decorator**: applied to all export functions that read weights (`unsloth_save_model`, `unsloth_generic_save`, etc.).

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Update `__all__` in `save.py` (line 48–54)

Add `"export_lora_to_onnx"` to the existing list:

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
    "export_lora_to_onnx",          # ← add this
]
```

---

### Step 2 — Add `export_lora_to_onnx()` module-level function (insert after line 2141, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

Location anchor: right after the closing of `save_lora_to_custom_dir` (the blank line at ~line 2141).

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def export_lora_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Export LoRA adapter weights from an Unsloth / PEFT model to ONNX format.

    The exported ONNX file is a *weight container*: it has no computation
    graph nodes, only ONNX initializers (named tensors) — one ``lora_A``
    and one ``lora_B`` entry per targeted module.  A JSON sidecar file
    (``lora_metadata.json``) is written alongside the ``.onnx`` file and
    contains the adapter hyper-parameters.

    Args:
        model: A PEFT-wrapped unsloth model (returned by
            ``FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model`` or equivalent).
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
            Created automatically if it does not exist.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Raises:
        ImportError: If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
        ValueError: If no trainable LoRA adapter layers are found in *model*.
    """
    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import numpy_helper, helper
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it with: pip install onnx"
        )
    import json

    output_dir = str(output_dir)
    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    # ── 1. Read LoRA hyper-parameters from peft_config ────────────────────
    rank           = None
    lora_alpha     = None
    target_modules = None

    if hasattr(model, "peft_config") and model.peft_config:
        adapter_name   = next(iter(model.peft_config))   # typically "default"
        peft_cfg       = model.peft_config[adapter_name]
        rank           = getattr(peft_cfg, "r",              None)
        lora_alpha     = getattr(peft_cfg, "lora_alpha",     None)
        target_modules = getattr(peft_cfg, "target_modules", None)
    pass

    # ── 2. Iterate all modules and collect LoRA A / B weights ─────────────
    initializers   = []   # ONNX TensorProto list
    layer_metadata = {}   # per-layer info for JSON sidecar

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        # Skip merged or disabled adapters (they have no separate A/B)
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", True) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        active = active[0]

        if not (hasattr(module, "lora_A") and active in module.lora_A):
            continue

        A_weight = module.lora_A[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        B_weight = module.lora_B[active].weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy()
        scale    = float(module.scaling[active])

        a_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_A.weight"
        b_tensor_name = f"{module_name}.lora_B.weight"

        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(A_weight, name=a_tensor_name))
        initializers.append(numpy_helper.from_array(B_weight, name=b_tensor_name))

        layer_metadata[module_name] = {
            "lora_A_shape" : list(A_weight.shape),   # (rank, in_features)
            "lora_B_shape" : list(B_weight.shape),   # (out_features, rank)
            "scale"        : scale,
        }
    pass

    if not initializers:
        raise ValueError(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers found in the model. "
            "Make sure the model was created with `get_peft_model()` and the "
            "adapters have not been merged."
        )
    pass

    # ── 3. Build minimal ONNX graph (no nodes — weight-container only) ────
    graph = helper.make_graph(
        nodes       = [],
        name        = "lora_adapters",
        inputs      = [],
        outputs     = [],
        initializer = initializers,
    )

    model_proto = helper.make_model(
        graph,
        opset_imports = [helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        ir_version    = 8,   # compatible with opset 17
    )
    model_proto.doc_string = (
        "LoRA adapter weights exported by Unsloth. "
        "Each initializer is a lora_A or lora_B weight matrix. "
        "See lora_metadata.json for hyper-parameters."
    )

    # ── 4. Embed metadata as ONNX model properties ────────────────────────
    meta_dict = {
        "lora_rank"      : str(rank),
        "lora_alpha"     : str(lora_alpha),
        "target_modules" : str(target_modules),
        "opset_version"  : str(opset_version),
        "framework"      : "unsloth",
    }
    for key, value in meta_dict.items():
        entry       = model_proto.metadata_props.add()
        entry.key   = key
        entry.value = value
    pass

    # ── 5. Save ONNX file ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    onnx_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_adapters.onnx")
    onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA ONNX weights to {onnx_path}")

    # ── 6. Save JSON metadata sidecar ─────────────────────────────────────
    full_metadata = {
        "lora_rank"      : rank,
        "lora_alpha"     : lora_alpha,
        "target_modules" : list(target_modules) if target_modules else None,
        "opset_version"  : opset_version,
        "layers"         : layer_metadata,
    }
    json_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_metadata.json")
    with open(json_path, "w") as fh:
        json.dump(full_metadata, fh, indent=2)
    print(f"Unsloth: Saved LoRA metadata to {json_path}")

    return onnx_path
pass
```

---

### Step 3 — Add `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx()` instance-method wrapper (insert after `export_lora_to_onnx`, before `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub`)

This thin wrapper mirrors the pattern of every other `unsloth_save_pretrained_*` function (compare `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao` at line 2502).

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> str:
    """Save LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    Wraps :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` as an instance method attached to the
    model by :func:`patch_saving_functions`.

    Args:
        output_dir (str | os.PathLike): Directory to write output files.
        opset_version (int, optional): ONNX opset version.  Defaults to 17.

    Returns:
        str: Absolute path to the saved ``lora_adapters.onnx`` file.

    Example::

        model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
        model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, ...)
        # ... fine-tune ...
        model.save_pretrained_onnx("my_lora_onnx/")
    """
    return export_lora_to_onnx(
        model         = self,
        output_dir    = output_dir,
        opset_version = opset_version,
    )
pass
```

---

## Design Decisions & Rationale

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Weight-container ONNX graph** (initializers, no nodes) | LoRA adapters are pure weight deltas, not standalone runnable graphs. This is the standard way to package tensors in ONNX without inventing a dummy inference graph. |
| **Float32 conversion** | ONNX doesn't natively represent quantized 4-bit weights; converting to fp32 ensures universal compatibility with ONNX Runtime and downstream tools. |
| **Lazy `import onnx`** | `onnx` is an optional dependency; failing at call-time (not import-time) is consistent with other optional deps in the file (torchao, huggingface_hub). |
| **JSON sidecar** | ONNX metadata_props stores only strings; a separate JSON file makes hyper-params easily accessible by downstream code without parsing ONNX. |
| **Single `.onnx` file** | All adapter layers in one file is simpler to manage than one file per layer. Layer names are embedded in tensor names. |
| **Both vision and non-vision branches** | `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` doesn't depend on model architecture; registering it for vision models is harmless and consistent. |

---

def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model

def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass

        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
